inline_comments.message:
The build failure that occurred with patchset 1 is apparently the result of a corrupt file on the Jenkins server.

I did run the Junit tests, and although they completed successfully, I don't think they are doing what you expect:

[INFO] Running org.onap.policy.m2.base.TransactionTest
03:07:48.636 [main] INFO org.onap.policy.m2.base.Transaction - Transaction constructor
03:07:48.649 [main] ERROR org.onap.policy.m2.base.Transaction - 'Transaction' can't find actor testActor
03:07:48.649 [main] ERROR org.onap.policy.m2.base.Transaction - 'Transaction' received unexpected timeout
03:07:48.649 [main] ERROR org.onap.policy.m2.base.Transaction - 'Transaction' received unexpected timeout
03:07:48.652 [main] INFO org.onap.policy.m2.base.Transaction - Transaction constructor

The 'Actor' mechanism makes use of 'java.util.ServiceLoader' to locate Actor implementations. You need to create a file called 'controlloop/m2/base/src/test/resources/META-INF/services/org.onap.policy.m2.base.Actor', which has the following contents:

org.onap.policy.m2.base.impl.ActorImpl

Perhaps you did create this file, but forgot to include it in the patch set.

The end result is that 'ActorImpl' and 'OperationImpl' are never referenced -- I tried the build after removing both files, and got the same result. The fact that the tests still "passed" suggests that more verification is needed. It seems to me that the tests would be more useful if you could verify that 'ActorImpl' and 'OperationImpl' were actually invoked where expected, and had the expected effects.
based on something I saw Ali doing, it appears that test method names should start with "test" instead of ending with "Test".
maybe add some tests for sunny day scenarios, too
missing a call to deliver?  and a check to see that the message was, indeed, delivered?
since this is a utility class, its constructor should be private, preventing this from compiling

I tried the "prop.put" option, as well as creating a minimal controller, and couldn't get around the "no reverse coder has been found" error. I may be missing something, or you may need a controller using dmaap or ueb.
